channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Facing Pressure, Trump Officials Reject Claims They’re Softening on Immigration
item.description: Immigration hard-liners have grown frustrated with the level of deportations and the Department of Homeland Security’s attempts, under its new secretary, to stay under the radar.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.media:description: Federal agents operating in Minneapolis in January. A growing share of Republicans now say the administration is doing too little on deportations, according to a poll released by Pew on Monday.

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil Hurtles Toward Potential Deportation as U.S. Speeds Case
item.description: A key judicial decision in Mr. Khalil’s immigration case was expedited significantly and included the recusal of multiple judges.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Nicholas Nehamas
item.category: United Nations Relief and Works Agency
item.media:description: Mahmoud Khalil, who became the face of President Trump’s crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters, is facing possible expulsion from the United States.

item.title: Trump Administration Wants to Strip 12 Immigrants of U.S. Citizenship
item.description: The targeted Americans are accused of misdeeds that can qualify them to lose citizenship, but denaturalization has rarely been invoked in the past.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Madeleine Ngo
item.media:description: A naturalization ceremony for new citizens in Chicago last year.

item.title: Lawmakers May Continue to Inspect ICE Detention Centers, Appeals Court Rules
item.description: A federal appeals court declined, for now, to halt an order requiring D.H.S. to permit unannounced visits to immigration detention centers by Democrats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Delaney Hall, a 1,000-person detention center used by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, in Newark, N.J.

item.title: Trump Plans to Fire F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Dr. Makary has been a supporter of the Make American Healthy Again Movement but made some enemies in the administration over vaping, the abortion pill and rejections of new drugs.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Maggie Haberman
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary, commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration, at the White House last year.

item.title: U.F.O. Files Released by U.S. Shed Light on What the Government Knows
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released online what it called “new, never-before-seen” files, dating back decades, related to unidentified flying objects.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An image of a U.F.O. resembling a football-shaped body near Japan that the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command reported.

item.title: U.S. Conducts Third Boat Strike in 5 Days, Killing 2 and Leaving a Survivor
item.description: Of the 57 attacks in the Caribbean and eastern Pacific against boats accused of drug trafficking, there have rarely been survivors.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
item.media:description: A recent image from a video provided by the U.S. military shows what it said was a strike on a boat in the Caribbean.

item.title: Trump Gave Out a No-Bid Contract to Turn D.C.’s Reflecting Pool Blue
item.description: President Trump handpicked a firm he said had worked on his swimming pool to repair the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times

item.title: Republicans Are Building an Advantage in Redistricting. How Much?
item.description: Where things stands in the race for House control after recent court rulings.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest/The New York Times
item.media:description: State Senator Charlane Oliver had a banner pulled from her hands as the Tennessee General Assembly gave approval to a congressional map intended to eliminate the state’s lone Democratic-held district.

item.title: Virginia Court Strikes Down Redistricted Voting Map in a Huge Blow to Democrats
item.description: The decision is a major victory for Republicans, wiping away a measure approved by voters to allow Democrats to gain as many as four House seats in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson, Nick Corasaniti and Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Steve Earley/The Virginian-Pilot, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The ruling from the Virginia Supreme Court leaves Democrats at a disadvantage in the country’s redistricting fight.

item.title: 4 Takeaways From the Virginia Supreme Court Gerrymandering Decision
item.description: The ruling was a major lift to the G.O.P. efforts to build a structural advantage through redistricting.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: Voters at a polling location in Arlington, Va., last month.

item.title: Alabama Asks Supreme Court to Allow it to Use New Voting Map
item.description: State officials urged the justices to allow them to jettison Alabama’s congressional district map, citing the Supreme Court’s recent decision that dealt a blow to the Voting Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane
item.media:description: State representatives voting on a redistricting bill during a special session of the Alabama Legislature on Wednesday.

item.title: David Attenborough, a Voice of Nature, Turns 100
item.description: Pictures and striking scenes from the making of perhaps the world’s most celebrated naturalist.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:credit: John Stillwell/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Mr. Attenborough enrolled at the University of Cambridge in 1945, where he studied natural sciences, including geology and zoology.

item.title: How China’s Leader Lost Faith in His Generals
item.description: Xi Jinping spent 13 years building a military to rival that of the United States. But the stronger the Chinese forces grew, the less he trusted the generals he had handpicked to run them.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, during a military parade in Tiananmen Square in 2025 in Beijing.

item.title: Why the Trump-Xi Summit Matters, Even if Little Seems to Come of It
item.description: The war in Iran, trade, artificial intelligence and Taiwan are expected to be on the agenda. But expectations are modest.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in Busan, South Korea, in 2025.

item.title: China’s Exports and Imports Set Records in April Amid High Energy Costs
item.description: The country’s exports surged and its trade surplus with the United States widened ahead of President Trump’s scheduled visit next week to Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the Beijing auto show in April, which featured hundreds of electric cars. Chinese electric vehicles are being exported in record numbers.

item.title: A Less Muscular Victory Day Parade Shows Putin’s Growing Vulnerability
item.description: The event, shrunken because of security fears, adds to a sense that Moscow and other cities can no longer be insulated from the war.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.media:description: Police officers in central Moscow before the parade on Saturday.

item.title: U.S. Targets Iran’s Missile and Drone Program With Sanctions
item.description: The new measures are focused on Chinese companies that supply Iran’s military with materials to make drones.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Friday.

item.title: Oil Slick Is Detected Off Kharg Island in the Persian Gulf
item.description: The slick, captured in satellite images near Kharg Island in the Persian Gulf, has raised concerns about the condition of Iran’s oil infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi, Mira Rojanasakul and Hiroko Tabuchi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An image from the European Space Agency’s Copernicus Sentinel-2 satellite showing a slick off Kharg Island on Wednesday.

item.title: Before Hantavirus Outbreak, the MV Hondius Attracted ‘a Different Type of Traveler’
item.description: Former passengers on the MV Hondius say that the ship was made for wildlife expeditions and that the crew took safety very seriously.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Claire Moses and Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:description: The MV Hondius off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday. Passengers on the ship showed symptoms of the hantavirus, and some died.

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Sickens 115 on Cruise Ship
item.description: More than 100 passengers and 13 crew members on the Caribbean Princess reported being ill, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: The Caribbean Princess left Port Everglades in Fort Lauderdale, Fla., on April 28 and is expected to return to Florida on Monday.

item.title: Compare the Purported Epstein Suicide Note to His Writings
item.description: The New York Times has not authenticated the note. But some of its phrases are similar or identical to ones Jeffrey Epstein used in emails and another handwritten letter.
item.dc:creator: Steve Eder and Eve Edelheit
item.media:credit: New York State Sex Offender Registry, via Associated Press

item.title: In Kristin Smart Case, Soil Suggests Human Remains Once Present in Yard
item.description: A California sheriff said investigators were searching the backyard of a woman whose son was convicted of murdering Ms. Smart, who went missing in 1996.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín
item.media:description: Scientists on Thursday took soil samples in Arroyo Grande, Calif.

item.title: 7 Busy Parents Share Their Quickest Go-To Dinners
item.description: End-of-school year chaos has arrived. These 20-minute (or less) meals will get you cooking through it.
item.dc:creator: Margaux Laskey
item.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini. Prop Stylist: Megan Hedgpeth.

item.title: We’re at the Dawn of the Ozempic Era — and It’s Really Weird
item.description: One in eight American adults is taking GLP-1s. What does that mean for how we relate to our food, bodies and one another?
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Annie Galvin

item.title: Hegseth Is Sending Us a Warning
item.description: The secretary of defense has argued that military retirees are subject to freedom of speech restrictions. Let that sink in.
item.dc:creator: Frank Kendall
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Man Dies in Subway Attack; Mamdani Orders Inquiry Into Suspect’s Release From Bellevue
item.description: A 76-year-old man died after he was pushed down subway stairs, and the police arrested a suspect who had been discharged from the public hospital’s psychiatric unit hours earlier.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Anusha Bayya
item.media:credit: an Rong Xu for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York called for an immediate investigation into the psychiatric evaluation and discharge of a man who the police said fatally assaulted another man five hours after his release.

item.title: Takeaways From the 2026 U.K. Local Elections
item.description: Results from municipal and regional elections signaled major gains for the right-wing populist Reform U.K. and steep losses for Labour.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: British Prime Minister and Labour leader Keir Starmer in London on Friday.

item.title: Blake Lively Settlement Had No Financial Payout, Filing Confirms
item.description: Justin Baldoni’s company did not agree to make a payout as part of the deal that ended Ms. Lively’s lawsuit, but she is able to seek compensation through a separate legal avenue.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: As the legal dispute between Justin Baldoni and Blake Lively continues, a new court filing confirms their settlement agreement includes no financial payout to her. But it enables her to seek compensation through an alternative legal avenue.

item.title: Proposed Rule by Postal Service Could Allow Handguns to Be Mailed
item.description: The proposal comes after the Justice Department said this year that a century-old law banning the practice was unconstitutional. Democratic leaders are calling it “unlawful.”
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Postal Service worker delivering a package to a home in the Bronx, New York.
